title: Day 08 Quiz — APIs, useEffect, and UI States
description: Verify fetch/async patterns, TypeScript API types, useEffect dependencies, and loading/error/empty state handling.

questions.prompt: fetch() automatically throws an error when the server responds with HTTP 404.
questions.explanation: fetch resolves successfully for 4xx/5xx responses. You must check response.ok or response.status manually.

questions.prompt: What does optional chaining weather?.main?.temp prevent?

options.text: API rate limiting
Runtime error when accessing properties on null or undefined
Infinite re-renders
CORS failures

questions.explanation: Optional chaining short-circuits to undefined instead of throwing when an intermediate value is nullish.

questions.prompt: When should you use useEffect for weather fetching?

options.text: For every fetch — never use event handlers
When you need to synchronize UI with an external system after render, e.g. when a query state changes
Only on class components
To replace useState entirely

questions.explanation: useEffect is for side effects that synchronize with external systems. Button-click fetch can live in a handler; refetch-on-query-change fits useEffect.

questions.prompt: Which cause infinite API calls in useEffect? Select all that apply.

options.text: No dependency array when the effect calls setState
Dependency array [query] when query only changes on submit
Dependency on an object literal created inline each render
Empty dependency array [] on mount-only fetch

questions.explanation: Missing deps with setState re-triggers every render. Inline object deps are new references every render. [] runs once; stable [query] runs when query changes.

questions.prompt: Check HTTP success with {{blank1}} before calling response.json(). Handle failures in a {{blank2}} block.
questions.explanation: response.ok is true for status 200–299. Async errors from fetch and thrown HTTP errors belong in catch.
Optional chaining returns undefined when data is null; nullish coalescing falls back to 0.

questions.prompt: Why show a loading skeleton instead of only a spinner?

options.text: Skeletons use less memory than spinners
Skeleton preserves layout and reduces perceived layout shift when data arrives
Google requires skeletons for SEO
useEffect only works with skeleton components

questions.explanation: Skeletons mirror final content shape — a core loading strategy in production dashboards.

questions.prompt: When would you choose fetch over Axios in a new React project?
questions.sampleAnswer: fetch is built into browsers — no extra dependency, smaller bundle, sufficient for straightforward JSON APIs. Axios adds interceptors, automatic JSON transform, and wider browser support — useful at scale but not required on day one.
questions.explanation: Interviewers want trade-offs, not fetch is always better.

questions.prompt: Complete the interface for nested weather data.
questions.explanation: Nested array types reference a named interface describing each element — matches OpenWeather-style responses.

options.text: A try/catch inside every useEffect
A React class or library component that catches render errors in children and shows fallback UI
A TypeScript interface for API errors
A CSS border around error messages

questions.explanation: Error boundaries catch errors during rendering in child trees — different from try/catch around async fetch. Deep implementation comes later; know the concept today.
